---
title: "Recursive Self-Improvement Boundaries"
chapter_id: "recursive-self-improvement-boundaries"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "scf"
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "rmi"
  - "alignment_field"
  - "viea"
  - "talos"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Recursive Self-Improvement Boundaries

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `recursive-self-improvement-boundaries` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `scf`, `benchmaxxing`, `rmi`, `alignment_field`, `viea`, `talos`, `moecot` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The book needs to state when self-improvement is allowed, what it may modify, and which invariants it cannot weaken.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Treating self-improvement as automatic capability growth ignores evaluator integrity, constitutional preservation, authority ceilings, and regression memory.

## Core claim

[recursive-self-improvement-boundaries.core, support: argument] Recursive self-improvement is allowed only as a bounded, evidence-gated, reversible transition over stable capability fields.

## Mechanism

- Define improvement proposals, protected invariants, and evidence gates.
- Separate proposing, evaluating, approving, committing, and monitoring.
- Delay autonomous replacement until evaluators and governance are credible.

## Interfaces

- SCFs define replaceable units.
- Evidence ratchets provide gates.
- Alignment supplies protected constraints.

## Invariants

- Self-improvement cannot weaken protected invariants.
- Evaluator independence is required.
- Every accepted change remains auditable.

## Failure modes

- Recursive evaluator capture.
- Constitutional weakening.
- Irreversible flawed upgrades.

## Minimal implementation

A self-improvement protocol that can reject, quarantine, roll back, or retire a proposed change.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Protected-invariant preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Evaluator independence test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Self-improvement rollback scenario | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `scf` | Stable Capability Fields | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Use public release v1.0 when available. Stable boundaries, replacement, bounded authority, recoverable evolution. |
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `alignment_field` | Field of God / Alignment Field family | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Primary Alignment Field draft found. Use with Ethica Mechanica and Eternal Code. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
